channel.title: Food on SuperList.us
channel.description: Recent content in Food on SuperList.us

item.title: Peculiar Winery
item.description: A family-owned winery in Peculiar, Missouri, run by a guy who went from professional wrestling to television to wine. Handcrafted Missouri wines in a town whose official slogan is “Where the Odds Are With You.” The tasting room is open on weekends and the name has never once needed explaining.

item.title: Keek's Treats & Café
item.description: A small café near the Boring Station Trailhead serving breakfast sandwiches, soups, cookies, brownies, and cinnamon rolls. Everything is made fresh and the portions are generous. The kind of place where the owner knows your name by your third visit.

item.title: Fox Meadows Creamery
item.description: Cow-to-cone ice cream made on-site with milk from their own small dairy farm. 24 flavors, a playground, a market with local foods, and an address that will make your GPS voice say something uncomfortable. Located in Intercourse, Pennsylvania, obviously.

item.title: Boring Brewing Company
item.description: Craft beer from Boring, Oregon. Every label writes itself. The town’s name is the best marketing department a brewery could ask for. Local, small-batch, and genuinely good — which is, frankly, a little on-the-nose for a town called Boring.

item.title: Sam & Ella's Chicken Palace
item.description: Multiple restaurants across the US have independently decided that naming a chicken restaurant after a food-borne illness is a power move. Say “Sam and Ella’s” out loud. Now say it faster. The food has good reviews, despite the name doing absolutely everything it can to undermine consumer confidence.

item.title: Donkey Balls Original Factory & Store
item.description: A chocolate company on the Big Island of Hawaii that sells chocolate-covered macadamia nuts called, yes, Donkey Balls. The “Dirty Balls” variety is coated in cocoa powder. The branding is ridiculous on purpose and it has worked spectacularly. Free factory tours available.

item.title: Not So Boring Bar & Grill
item.description: A bar in Boring, Oregon, that decided to address the elephant in the room with its name. Live music, trivia nights, and a menu that proves small-town bars can do more than frozen mozzarella sticks. The name does the marketing for them.

item.title: Kitchen Kettle Village
item.description: Over 40 local shops in one walkable village in the heart of Intercourse, Pennsylvania. Homemade jams, Amish quilts, handcrafted leather goods, and fresh shoofly pie. The name of the town gets all the attention, but the shopping keeps people coming back. Family-run since 1954.

item.title: Boring Bean Coffee
item.description: A coffee roaster in Boring, Oregon, that is anything but boring. Started when they began roasting beans outside the door of their landscaping supply business, Boring Bark. Customers started showing up more for the coffee than the mulch. Fair trade, small batch, genuinely great coffee from a town that insists on underselling itself.
